First, the problem says that Janet’s ducks lay 16 eggs per day. That seems straightforward. So, every day, she has 16 fresh duck eggs.

 
Next, she eats three eggs for breakfast every morning. Hmm, so out of the 16 eggs she lays, she uses 3 for herself. That means she has 16 - 3 = 13 eggs left after she eats her breakfast.

Okay, so I need to figure out how far John is from home after his entire trip. Let me try to break this down step by step.

First, John drives for 3 hours at 60 mph. Hmm, so I can calculate the distance he covers in those 3 hours. Distance is speed multiplied by time, right? So that would be 60 mph * 3 hours. Let me do that math: 60 * 3 is 180. So, he goes 180 miles away from home in the first part.

Then, he turns around because he forgot something at home. Now, he tries to get home in 4 hours.
